reviews.title: Bad experience
reviews.text:
First, upon my arrival, the receptionist told me they were sorry but couldn't accommodate any guests for a limited time and would transfer me to another nearby hotel. I didn't object and was transferred. Check-in was fairly easy until I checked out. That's when the provocation and inexperience of the receptionists began. First, they said, "Sir, there's a problem with some items in the minibar." I replied that I hadn't used anything from the minibar and spoke to another employee. Then, a different employee, clearly unsuited to be handling guests, came and told me, "I apologize, my colleague made a mistake. We need your payment for your stay!" I asked her how that was possible since I had already paid in full online and there seemed to be an error with the previous hotel. She told me to pay now and check my payments with the app I booked through. What nonsense! This only increased my frustration and raised my voice until she apologized, saying, "We're using an iPhone; there was a mistake on our end. Thank you for your stay!"
What is the NIGHTMARE!!
reviews.response:
Dear Al Mamoon,

Thank you for your feedback, and we sincerely apologize for the inconvenience you experienced. We regret the confusion during check-out, especially regarding the minibar and incorrect payment request. 

Your comments have been shared with the team to improve our service. We truly appreciate your patience and are sorry for the frustration caused.

Best regards,
Mohammed Nabil
Assistant Front Office Manager

reviews.title: The Worst Hotel Experience Ever – Completely Disappointed
reviews.positives:
The Worst Hotel Experience Ever – Completely Disappointed

I don’t usually write reviews, but this was by far the worst hotel experience I’ve ever had, and people need to be warned. From start to finish, everything about this place was a disaster.

Let’s start with customer service – absolutely terrible. Calling from the room was useless because no one answers. When someone finally picks up after ages, they’re completely unhelpful. One staff member, Jimmanq or Jimma (not sure about the spelling), had the audacity to hang up on me while I was talking. When I called again and it went back to her, she lied and claimed she transferred the call. Unbelievable. That level of disrespect is unacceptable.

Housekeeping? Slightly better in terms of answering the phone, but don’t get too hopeful. They either forget you called or bring the wrong items. At least they tried, unlike customer service, but still – not what you’d expect from any decent hotel.

Room maintenance was another nightmare. The lights weren’t even connected properly. We had to call an engineer just to turn them off at night from the main switch. Then call again in the morning to turn them on. Seriously?

And don’t even get me started on early check-in. I was told to arrive by 12 PM. I drove all the way from Dubai, got there early – and was given the room at 2:45 PM. If the room isn’t ready, why ask me to come early? Do you think people enjoy wasting their time and energy?

The only positive note in this whole experience was Manager Mohammed – a respectful and professional person who seems to actually care. I hope he can bring some serious improvement, because the rest of the staff and service standards were absolutely unacceptable.

Also, it was pretty obvious the hotel staff treat certain guests differently. If you’re not part of the segment they’re clearly focused on, don’t expect decent service. It was disappointing and very telling.

This was my first and last time staying here.

reviews.title: Honest Review
reviews.text:
We were prepared to leave Pullman Al Marjan quietly, but the reception team’s question about how our stay went prompted me to share our experience for everyone’s awareness.

We booked three rooms a month in advance, prioritizing convenience as we usually do. We arrived at 2:00 PM and were informed that the rooms would be ready shortly. We paid the AED 20 per room and waited.

Shortly after, the receptionist approached us to inform us—rather abruptly—that our requests could not be accommodated. Our request was simple: for the rooms to be next to each other, and for at least one room to be decorated as we were celebrating a birthday. The response from her and her manager, also at the reception, was a repeated “No” and “Not possible.”

We then asked if, at the very least, a courtesy upgrade could be offered in recognition of our special occasion. Again, the response was an immediate “No,” delivered with a demeanor that felt dismissive and unwelcoming. It is disheartening that individuals with such attitudes represent a hotel and resort chain with an otherwise reputable name.

Eventually, we were given one upgraded room—yet we still waited three more hours for the remaining two rooms to be assigned.

Overall, the experience left us feeling disappointed and embarrassed to have chosen this hotel. With the level of service we encountered, I can confidently say we will not be returning for another stay.
reviews.response:
Dear Angela,

Thank you for sharing your feedback regarding your recent experience at Pullman Resort Al Marjan Island. I sincerely apologize for the challenges you faced during your check-in process and the handling of your requests. It is our aim to provide a welcoming atmosphere, especially during special occasions like birthdays, and I regret that we did not meet your expectations in this regard.

While we strive to accommodate all guest requests, there are instances when we are unable to do so due to various constraints. I acknowledge how disappointing it must have been to receive a negative response regarding your room arrangements and the requested upgrade. Additionally, the delay in room assignment is not reflective of our usual service standards, and I appreciate your patience during this time.

Your comments will be shared with our team to ensure that we learn from this feedback. Thank you for bringing these matters to our attention, and I hope you will consider giving us anot

reviews.title: Caution for Families with Small Children
reviews.text:
We arrived Thursday night.
My kids and I went for dinner at the international buffet restaurant, the only one open.
There was no warning of nuts in the food and my daughter had the chicken biryani which had nuts in it.

She developed an immediate and extremely severe reaction.
The hotel says doctor on call.

When I requested for a doctor they said it will be an extra charge to which I immediately agreed but then the doctor called me and refused to come and instead said to take my daughter to a certain hospital.

This was extremely stressful and dangerous as my daughter's condition was very bad and I am a stranger to the city without a car.

I requested for a hotel car/limo which they said they don't have.
Finally I rushed to the hospital emergency in a taxi.

Not stating that a dish has nuts in it is extremely risky for kids and a huge oversight. Furthermore the doctor is just not really on call as when I was telling him to come as it was an emergency he didn't.

Service in the Italian restaurant was terrible. half out table finished our food and didn't get served drinks, the other half had only drinks and no food given to them.
They also told me they didn't have any diet pepsi or diet coke.

How can a 5 star hotel run out of diet pepsi?

I will not be returning ever as a huge risk for my kids.

reviews.title: Wouldn't prefer to come back.
reviews.positives:
Despite all the issues we faced, the saving grace of our stay was Leila, the housekeeping manager. She went above and beyond to provide support and assistance, despite the troubles she herself was encountering with the hotel's operations. Her dedication and efforts deserve special recognition.
In an attempt to compensate for the inconveniences we endured, the hotel offered us a complimentary breakfast, which was a small gesture of goodwill. However, it did not make up for the overall disappointment and wasted time during our stay.
reviews.negatives:
Our recent stay at this hotel was unfortunately marred by a series of issues that made our experience far from enjoyable. From the moment we checked in, it was clear that there were significant communication problems between the staff, which directly impacted the quality of our stay.

The first problem we encountered was with the wifi. We were informed by the engineering team that only two people could use the wifi in a three-bedroom suite, which was quite baffling. The staff assured us that they would look into the matter, but when the night manager arrived, she simply shrugged off the problem, citing that the wifi was connected from France and due to high occupancy, there was nothing they could do about it. This left us disappointed and disconnected during our stay.

In addition to the wifi issue, our room had multiple other problems. At the beginning of our stay, there was no hot water, making morning showers an unpleasant experience. The fridge in the room was also not functioning, which meant we couldn't store any perishable items or enjoy a cold drink after a long day. To add to our woes, the water coming from the bathroom taps had a strange yellow tinge, raising concerns about its safety and cleanliness.

Throughout our stay, we found that there was a considerable lack of coordination and communication among the hotel staff. It took multiple complaints and considerable time and effort to get our room changed on the second day. Although they eventually managed to accommodate our request, it was a hassle that could have been easily avoided with better organization and responsiveness.

reviews.title: Stay was disappointing and the property is no longer what it used to be.
reviews.positives: Nothing. My previous 4 stays at this property—back when it was Al Marjan Resort, brought me back again, hoping the hospitality would be the same, if not better.
reviews.negatives: Frankly, apart from being familiar with the property, there’s little I can say I liked about this stay. The hotel is in urgent need of a serious renovation. Within a couple of hours of check-in, the power went out, and it took 30–40 minutes for an engineer to arrive. The bathroom has no exhaust fan, so it starts smelling almost immediately. The electric kettle’s plug didn’t match the socket beside it, a basic oversight. The staff were not particularly friendly, and the breakfast was below average. A friend in another room requested a toothbrush and toothpaste in the morning, and it took over an hour for it to be delivered. This property seems well past its golden days. I wouldn’t choose to stay here again. Wishing the hotel the best moving forward.

reviews.title: One of the Worst Stays I’ve Ever Had – Extremely Disappointing
reviews.positives:
One of the Worst Stays I’ve Ever Had – Extremely Disappointing

This was honestly one of the worst trips I’ve experienced in the UAE, especially in Ras Al Khaimah. The hotel room lacked even the most basic facilities – not even a toothbrush was provided. The bed was extremely uncomfortable, it felt like sleeping on the ground. My family was frustrated and disappointed, and we ended up spending the whole day outside just to avoid being at the hotel, only returning to sleep.

The breakfast experience was chaotic and stressful. It felt more like a fish market than a hotel buffet – no organization, no queue system, and zero staff presence to manage the crowd. I had two arguments with other guests just trying to get food, as no one respected any lines. I consider myself lucky to have waited only 45 minutes for two omelets, while others seemed like they were waiting for hours. Only one staff member was handling a line of more than 15 guests, which is completely unacceptable. Even if breakfast was free, guests deserve to be treated with respect and basic service standards.

It was very clear that the hotel was only interested in making money during the Eid holiday, cutting corners everywhere and offering the lowest possible level of service. I will absolutely never stay here again and wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone – not even to my worst enemy.

reviews.title: Great hotel
reviews.text:
The hotel is located at man-made Marjan Island.  It is a great destination for a weekend getaway.  Our stay was wonderful except for some incidents.  While watching an MTV our internet was disconnected.  When we called the front desk they told us that the internet was disconnected because we needed to put a deposit.  This should have been required during check in.  I told them that there was no need to cut the internet connection.  They could have just called us and required the deposit.

Around 3am, we finally decided to sleep but we found out that the lights would not turn off.  We called the front desk and they suggested to send a technician to fix it. The middle switch near the bed headboard was broken.  Since it was around 4am already so we opted to just try to sleep with the lights on and declined the service of the technician.  It was a bad decision, my companion was not able to sleep at all.

Overall the stay was great and wonderful.
